name: skannanios
description: Curated Claude Code skills & agents for AI-assisted software engineering.

plugins.name: taskforce
plugins.description: Multi-agent orchestration that drives a task from vague request to verified result: scout -> mastermind -> parallel hackers -> adversarial lookouts -> bounded fix-loop -> plain-language report, with promote-on-green so unverified code never touches your tree.

plugins.name: how-to-test
plugins.description: Analyze a repo and generate a step-by-step guide for manually testing it by hand — fresh clone to actually using the SDK/app and confirming it works. Covers prerequisites, dependencies, local setup, a hands-on walkthrough, and teardown. Not the automated test suite.

plugins.name: pr-description
plugins.description: Generate a pull-request description from the current branch's diff and commits: auto-detects the base branch, fills the repo's own PR template (or a sensible default), writes a Conventional Commits title, and never drops or reorders template sections.

plugins.name: commit-splitter
plugins.description: Split a messy working tree into clean, atomic commits with Conventional Commits messages: groups related changes together even across files, and separates unrelated changes into different commits even within one file (hunk-level staging). Proposes a commit-by-commit plan and only commits after you approve; never pushes.

plugins.name: teach-me
plugins.description: Learn any concept from a patient expert tutor: pick your level (noob/junior/senior/professional), get a plain-English step-by-step explanation that pauses for your questions, optionally learn by building a small runnable project, then multiple-choice understanding checks and a recap cheat-sheet to keep.
